title: Software Engineer
description:
**Job Description**
 We are looking for a Software Engineer with:
   

  

* 2\\+ years of professional experience.
* B.E./B.Tech. in Computer Science or a related technical discipline, or equivalent practical experience.
* Hands\\-on Linux user/admin basics: command line, packages (dnf/yum), services, logs, file permissions, simple network troubleshooting.
* Interest in Red Hat Enterprise Linux and/or Rocky Linux (EL8\\+); willingness to learn lifecycle and patching rhythm.
* Scripting: Python and/or shell—able to extend existing scripts and write small utilities with review.
* Containers: basic Docker or Podman (build, run, inspect layers, simple Dockerfiles).
* CI exposure is a plus: following pipeline logs, artifacts, reproducing failures.
* Quality mindset: can execute test checklists, improve docs, and keep runbooks accurate.
* Strong learning orientation, attention to detail, and communication; comfortable asking questions; works well in global / remote teams.


**How You Will Contribute And What You Will Learn**
* Implement and maintain automation and small features under senior direction (build steps, checks, reporting hooks).
* Run and extend validation for container base images and Linux workflows; document results.
* Triage routine issues: reproduce failures, gather logs, propose fixes for review.
* Update documentation and examples (install paths, mirror usage, common pitfalls).
* Support internal consumers with clear, scoped answers; escalate complex lifecycle, subscription, or compliance topics.
* Participate in code reviews and team rituals; grow toward independent ownership of a well\\-bounded component.


**Key Skills And Experience**
* Linux fundamentals; interest in RHEL\\-class / Rocky\\-class systems.
* Python and/or shell scripting (beginner to intermediate).
* Docker/Podman basics.
* Git and collaborative development.
* CI familiarity is a plus (Jenkins or similar).
* Communication and teamwork; eagerness to learn.

title: Software Engineer
description:
**Job Description**
**Family Description**
 Applied R\\&D (AR) consists of delving deeply into the open source software and how they can be translated into deliverable components on various clouds like AWS, OCP, Azure etc \\- all public clouds and Nokia Cloud environments. The components will be integrated by all Nokia Products for their specific use case.
   

  

**Subfamily Description**
 Software (SWA) comprises the definition, specification, and allocation of requirements from different sources utilising knowledge of systems engineering processes (specification \\& architecture). Contains processing of use case and feature requirements into conceptual models, operational scenarios, technical requirements, and functional description. Covers specification, design, implementation, and unit testing of Software according to the requirements and architecture defined in the systems engineering process. Covers establishment and maintenance of Software Configuration Management (SCM) practices into software development projects, continuously building and integrating infrastructure tools and systems.
   

  

**How You Will Contribute And What You Will Learn**
* Delivers SW / HW build\\-controlled productions releases (EG main and update releases, service packages, maintenance updates, and customer design engineering).
* Applies and maintains quality standards. Participates in process and tools evolutions and improvements.
* Applies the SW Care process (especially emergency case handling) contributing when needed to the fastest problem restoration.


**Key Skills And Experience**
* An Engineering graduate or equivalent degree with 3\\+ years of expertise in one or more programming/scripting environments. like C\\+\\+, Python.
* Deep knowledge on messaging app \\- Envoy . Should be able to understand the open source code, contribute to the open source.
* Should have very good debugging knowledge at networking, operating system levels details container and node levels.
* Exposure in DevOps, development in a Jenkins\\-based environment
* Expertise in container management environments such as K8S. Experience with dockers, HELM charts


**It would be nice if you also had:**
* Desirable to have demonstrated open\\-source contributions/penchant for open\\-source contributions, awareness on licensing, security \\& compliance aspects.
* Excellent communication skills, both verbal \\& written desired.

title: Software Engineer
description:
**Job Description**
 As a Lead Engineer, you will be a key member of the MI AN PE CSF 4LS department, taking on a role in addressing field\\-reported issues and customer deployments related to the CSF portfolio. Your primary focus will be on troubleshooting complex, multi\\-component issues and collaborating with development teams to identify root causes and implement effective solutions. This role requires a unique blend of technical depth and excellent communication skills to ensure timely resolution of customer\\-impacting problems and maintain strong relationships with both internal and external stakeholders.
   

  

**How You Will Contribute And What You Will Learn**
 Lead and provide hands\\-on support for field\\-reported issues and customer deployments across the CSF portfolio.
   

  

* Troubleshoot complex issues spanning multiple components, utilizing strong technical expertise.
* Collaborate closely with development teams for root cause analysis and effective problem\\-solving.
* Ensure timely resolution of customer\\-impacting problems, maintaining high standards of customer satisfaction.
* Communicate effectively with customers and internal stakeholders across different geographies and time zones.
* Document and report on issue resolution processes, providing clear and concise updates.
* Stay updated with the latest industry trends and technologies relevant to the CSF domain.
* Mentor and guide junior team members, fostering a culture of knowledge sharing and continuous improvement.
* Participate in on\\-call rotations to provide timely support for critical issues as needed.


**Key Skills And Experience**
* Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
* 5\\+ years of experience in software engineering or a similar technical role, with a focus on cloud\\-based solutions.
* Strong technical depth in one or more domain areas like OpenSearch, Fluentd, and OpenSearch Dashboards or Metrics collection and visualization with Prometheus and Grafana or Maria DB , Apache Kafka, RabbitMQ.
* Excellent problem\\-solving and analytical skills, with a track record of successful issue resolution.
* Clear and effective communication skills, both written and verbal, to interact with diverse stakeholders.
* Ability to work independently and manage multiple tasks simultaneously in a fast\\-paced environment.
* Experience with cloud technologies and platforms, such as AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud.
* Familiarity with containerization technologies (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes).
* Knowledge of agile development methodologies and practices is an asset.
* Willingness to learn and adapt to new technologies and industry trends.

title: Software Engineer
description:
**Company Background** 




 Ownwell helps property owners reduce the costs of owning real estate. Our proprietary software automatically identifies property owners that are overpaying on real estate expenses. We then manage the end\\-to\\-end process of reducing bills through tax appeals, exemptions, and corrections.
 



 Over $40 billion is overpaid in property taxes every year, and inaccurate tax assessments disproportionately affect people of color, immigrants, as well as low\\-income communities.
 



 We’re dedicated to making the costs of property ownership more transparent and equitable. We believe that regardless of status or level of real estate expertise, everyone should have access to the information, tools, and resources to manage their real estate with confidence.
 



 Ownwell is well\\-funded and venture\\-backed by some of the best investors in the world. Our customer base has grown by more than 1000% year\\-over\\-year with exceptional feedback demonstrating clear product market fit. We are looking for driven and passionate team members who thrive in a collaborative, positive culture where we all win together. If this sounds like the place for you, come help us change the way everyday homeowners manage their real estate across the country.
 



**Our Culture** 




 People are our superpower! Centered in everything we do is a true sense of team. We listen and we learn from each other. We are on this rocketship together and embrace a fast\\-paced, truly collaborative environment. We are here to win as a team and as a company.
 



 We’ve brought together General Appraisers, Certified Public Accountants, Property Tax Consultants, Data scientists, PhDs, best\\-in\\-class customer support representatives, and more to deliver top results for our customers.
 



 Our core values are our guiding principles in everything we do
 


* Customer Obsession
* Take Ownership
* Do The Right Thing
* Go Far Together
* Accelerate Innovation



  





  





**The Role** 
 :
 



 As a Software Engineer on the Spyder Team at Ownwell, you'll develop software solutions to improve operational efficiency and procure real time real estate data. The Spyder Team sits at the core of the company, serving as the foundation for all Ownwell services. Your work will contribute to all aspects of the company, ensuring accuracy and availability of Ownwell systems. If you're passionate about building software solutions to address complex problems, then this is the role for you
 *!* 




*This role will be based in Gurugram, India* 




  





**Responsibilities** 



* Spearheading multi\\-source data procurement processes of real time real estate data
* Building software that streamlines complex business \\& bureaucratic processes
* Developing scalable internal solutions to ensure system accuracy and availability
* Collaborating with stakeholders from Engineering, Product, Operations, Customer Care, and Marketing



**Requirements** 



* 2\\+ years of experience in software development
* Strong backend and operational automation skills
* Experience with Selenium Webdriver
* Experience with Big Data
* Experience with Python, SQL, \\& AWS

title: Software Development Engineer - DevOps
description:
**Your work days are brighter here.**
 We’re obsessed with making hard work pay off, for our people, our customers, and the world around us. As a Fortune 500 company and a leading AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, we’re shaping the future of work so teams can reach their potential and focus on what matters most. The minute you join, you’ll feel it. Not just in the products we build, but in how we show up for each other. Our culture is rooted in integrity, empathy, and shared enthusiasm. We’re in this together, tackling big challenges with bold ideas and genuine care. We look for curious minds and courageous collaborators who bring sun\\-drenched optimism and drive. Whether you're building smarter solutions, supporting customers, or creating a space where everyone belongs, you’ll do meaningful work with Workmates who’ve got your back. In return, we’ll give you the trust to take risks, the tools to grow, the skills to develop and the support of a company invested in you for the long haul. So, if you want to inspire a brighter work day for everyone, including yourself, you’ve found a match in Workday, and we hope to be a match for you too.
   

  

**About The Team**
 Workday is a global leader in enterprise cloud applications for HR and Finance, and our Core Services team is the engine that keeps it running. We don’t just manage infrastructure; we build the foundational distributed systems' platform that powers mission\\-critical financial and HR data for the world’s largest organizations.
   

  

**About The Role**
 We are looking for a DevOps \\& Platform Engineer to join our Core Services team. In this role, you won’t just manage servers—you will build the high\\-velocity engine that powers Workday. You’ll be responsible for designing and scaling our hybrid\\-cloud infrastructure across private and public cloud including AWS and GCP, ensuring our core platforms are resilient, secure, and lightning\\-fast.
   

  

 Your mission is Developer Enablement: creating the seamless CI/CD pipelines and automated environments that allow our global engineering teams to innovate without friction. If you are passionate about "Infrastructure as Code" and thrive on solving complex distributed systems challenges, this is the place for you.
   

  

****What You’ll Do****
****🏗️ Cloud Architecture \\& Orchestration****
* Design \\& Optimize: Build and refine complex cloud solutions on AWS/GCP, focusing on high\\-performance compute, storage, caching, and networking.
* Master the Cluster: Deploy and scale applications using Docker and Kubernetes, ensuring our enterprise\\-scale clusters achieve maximum availability and resource efficiency.
* Automate Everything: Architect and maintain Infrastructure as Code (IaC) to automate the entire lifecycle of our cloud resources.


****🚀 CI/CD \\& Developer Experience****
* Modernize Delivery: Build advanced CI/CD pipelines and GitOps workflows to drive world\\-class release automation.
* Empower Engineers: Serve as a Platform SME, helping development teams streamline their local builds, troubleshoot deployments, and master software configuration.


****🛡️ Reliability \\& Security****
* Drive Observability: Implement proactive monitoring, logging, and alerting to detect and resolve infrastructure bottlenecks before they impact customers.
* Harden the Platform: Enforce security best practices and compliance standards across our cloud environments.
* Solve Complex Problems: Lead root\\-cause analysis (RCA) and provide expert\\-level support for platform\\-related incidents, collaborating across teams to build long\\-term fixes.
* Document the Future: Maintain high\\-quality documentation for infrastructure designs and operational playbooks to ensure team\\-wide alignment.


**About You**
****What You’ll Bring (Basic Qualifications)****
* Experience: 3\\+ years of professional experience in DevOps, Build Engineering, or Release Automation.
* Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent practical experience.
* Cloud Proficiency: Hands\\-on experience with AWS or GCP (Compute, Networking, and Storage).
* Container Mastery: Deep experience with Docker and Kubernetes orchestration at an enterprise scale.
* SRE Mindset: A solid understanding of Site Reliability Engineering principles, including monitoring, alerting, and fault analysis.
* The Java Stack: Foundational knowledge of Java and the JVM. You should be comfortable supporting Java\\-based apps and using build tools like Maven or Gradle.
* Automation First: Strong scripting skills in Python, Bash, or Go to automate repetitive tasks and infrastructure.


****The "Extra Credit" (Preferred Skills \\& Experience)****
* CI/CD Tooling: Experience with the modern "GitOps" stack: Jenkins, Helm, Argo CD/Workflows, and Kustomize.
* Infrastructure as Code (IaC): Proven ability to manage environments using Terraform or CloudFormation.
* Observability: Familiarity with keeping systems healthy using Prometheus, Grafana, CloudWatch, or the ELK stack.
* Build Systems: A passion for designing and maintaining scalable software build systems and managing artifacts via Artifactory or Nexus.
* Certifications: While not required, we value certifications like CKA/CKAD, AWS DevOps Engineer, or GCP Associate Cloud Engineer.
* Collaboration: A track record of working effectively with developers and architects to implement Public Cloud best practices.


**Our Approach to Flexible Work**
 With Flex Work, we’re combining the best of both worlds: in\\-person time and remote. Our approach enables our teams to deepen connections, maintain a strong community, and do their best work. We know that flexibility can take shape in many ways, so rather than a number of required days in\\-office each week, we simply
 **spend at least half (50%) of our time each quarter in the office or in the field** 
 with our customers, prospects, and partners (depending on role). This means you'll have the freedom to create a flexible schedule that caters to your business, team, and personal needs, while being intentional to make the most of time spent together. Those in our remote "home office" roles also have the opportunity to come together in our offices for important moments that matter.
   

  

 At Workday, we are committed to providing an accessible and inclusive hiring experience where all candidates can fully demonstrate their skills. If you require assistance or an accommodation at any point, please email accommodations@workday.com.
   

  

**Are you being referred to one of our roles? If so, ask your connection at Workday about our Employee Referral process!**
 At Workday, we value our candidates’ privacy and data security. Workday will never ask candidates to apply to jobs through websites that are not Workday Careers.
   

  

 Please be aware of sites that may ask for you to input your data in connection with a job posting that appears to be from Workday but is not.
   

  

 In addition, Workday will never ask candidates to pay a recruiting fee, or pay for consulting or coaching services, in order to apply for a job at Workday.

title: Software Engineer II
description:
**Job Description** 




 PMC is one of Pearson’s global technology platforms, globally distributed instances, multi\\-tenant, driving the digital transformation for eCommerce and web content. PMC currently processes more than 90% of global Pearson eCommerce revenue and serves over 80% of all Pearson web traffic.
 



 We currently run large content \\& eCommerce platforms serving the full\\-stack, but we want to start to move towards the next\\-generation of architectures – headless, API\\-first, cloud\\-native and micro\\-service based. Join a high performing team within a highly specialized technology department which focuses on delivery and transformation!
   

  





**About the job** 




 The right person will be 3 to 5 years’ experience in micro services development and AWS cloud. They will contribute to Pearson's technical transformation by developing global capability onto the PMC core technical platform. We are looking for someone with passion for technology and a drive to help us move towards our architectural vision.
 



 This is a unique opportunity for a keen technologist to help us build something that will directly enhance the lives of millions, by supporting the provision of education on a global scale.
   

  





**Key Responsibilities** 



  

  
* Design, implement, document, and unit test complex modules using
 **React, Java, Spring Boot, and Node.js**
* Develop and maintain RESTful APIs and microservices
* Ensure code quality, scalability, performance, and adherence to non\\-functional requirements
* Contribute to technical standards, best practices, and architectural discussions
* Collaborate with Product Owners, Project Managers, and Business Analysts to manage evolving requirements
* Work closely with QA teams to ensure high\\-quality releases
* Support troubleshooting, logging, and production monitoring activities
  


  


**Skills \\& Abilities:** 



  

  
* 3–5 years of experience in full\\-stack development
* Strong experience with:
* React
* Java
* Spring Boot
* Node.js
* REST API development
* AWS (including Lambda)
* Experience working in microservice\\-based architectures
* Strong understanding of:
* Object\\-oriented programming
* Design patterns
* Relational databases
* Complex integration ecosystems
* Non\\-functional requirements (performance, security, scalability)
* Hands\\-on experience leveraging GitHub Copilot for efficient application development
* Experience with logging, monitoring, and troubleshooting tools
* Strong communication and collaboration skills
* Interest in eCommerce platforms and digital commerce ecosystems
  


  


**Who we are:** 




 At Pearson, our purpose is simple: to help people realize the life they imagine through learning. We believe that every learning opportunity is a chance for a personal breakthrough. We are the world's lifelong learning company. For us, learning isn't just what we do. It's who we are. To learn more: We are Pearson.
 



 Pearson is an Equal Opportunity Employer and a member of E\\-Verify. Employment decisions are based on qualifications, merit and business need. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, ethnicity, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, national origin, protected veteran status, disability status or any other group protected by law. We actively seek qualified candidates who are protected veterans and individuals with disabilities as defined under VEVRAA and Section 503 of the Rehabilitation Act.
 



 If you are an individual with a disability and are unable or limited in your ability to use or access our career site as a result of your disability, you may request reasonable accommodations by emailing TalentExperienceGlobalTeam@grp.pearson.com.

**Key Responsibilities**
**Core Development (Go \\& Edge Systems)**
* Design and develop high\\-performance services in Go (Golang) running on edge appliances
* Build reliable systems for content download, validation, and duplication across devices
* Handle intermittent connectivity, retries, and fault tolerance in real\\-world network conditions
* Debug and resolve complex issues across distributed appliance environments


**Edge Appliance \\& System\\-Level Engineering**
* Develop software that interacts closely with OS, disk, and hardware layers
* Optimize disk I/O, file handling, and storage utilization for large media/content files
* Work with file systems such as XFS, ZFS, EXT4 for performance and reliability
* Ensure system stability during OS upgrades, hardware differences, and field deployments


**Distributed Systems \\& Fleet Management**
* Build and maintain systems operating across a 10K\\+ fleet
* Implement efficient content distribution, synchronization, and replication mechanisms
* Ensure data consistency and integrity across distributed nodes
* Design for scalability, observability, and remote operability of edge devices


**AWS \\& Cloud Integration**
* Integrate appliance workflows with AWS services (S3, EC2, CloudFront, CloudWatch)

* Optimize download throughput, cost, and reliability across large\\-scale deployments

**Performance \\& Reliability Engineering**

* Monitor and tune CPU, memory, disk I/O, and network utilization
* Identify bottlenecks and improve throughput, latency, and resilience
* Build systems that can self\\-heal and recover gracefully from failures


**Automation \\& Observability**

* Implement logging, metrics, and alerting for fleet\\-wide visibility
* Build automation using Go, Python, or Shell scripting

**Required Skills**
* 6\\+ years of software development experience

* Experience with performance tuning and system\\-level optimization


**Why This Role is Interesting**

* Solve challenges involving large\\-scale content distribution and edge computing
* Own critical systems that directly impact content delivery in theatre environments
* Opportunity to work across cloud \\+ edge \\+ system\\-level layers

title: Principal Technical Specialist
description:
**Job Description**
 We are looking for a Senior Software Engineer with:
   

  

* 12\\+ years of professional experience (or 6\\+ with a strong, demonstrable Linux/platform track record—align to your band).
* Expert\\-level Linux on Red Hat Enterprise Linux and/or Rocky Linux (EL8\\+): lifecycles, patching strategy, guest\\-level patterns, full vs maintenance phases, kernel backport behavior, Application Streams planning for Python, Java, and similar stacks.
* B.E./B.Tech. in Computer Science or equivalent; or equivalent depth via experience.
* Ownership of container base image programs: baseline package strategy, immutable vs floating tagging policy, rebuild vs no\\-rebuild release rules, and consumer migration planning.
* Security leadership: vulnerability triage at scale, prioritization with asset/vulnerability management, and pragmatic remediation roadmaps.
* Open\\-source compliance: credible engagement with legal/compliance and license workflows for distributed bases.
* CI/CD architecture: central CI, hermetic/restricted builds (no direct internet), reproducibility, and pipeline hardening.
* Repository \\& mirror strategy: RPM ecosystems, dnf/yum, internal mirrors, artifact repositories; clarity on subscription, vaulted content, and extended update models (what is centrally available vs locally procured).
* Mentoring: grows junior engineers; reviews designs and automation; improves runbooks and documentation as products.
* Stakeholder management: aligns architecture, security, program management, and product teams; communicates risk, trade\\-offs, and deprecations clearly.
* Excellent debugging and systems thinking; strong written and verbal English; global / remote collaboration.


**How You Will Contribute And What You Will Learn**
* Define and evolve common container base image strategy (baselines, cadence, tagging, traceability, release notes) with architecture and key consumers.
* Lead vulnerability and compliance work for base images: processes, exceptions, and prioritized fix paths.
* Own technical direction for Linux distribution enablement documentation and operational patterns (guest scope, multi\\-distro usage, mirrors, template\\-based image creation).
* Drive CI improvements for restricted environments: security, speed, observability, and fewer manual steps.
* Represent the team in cross\\-org forums; resolve ambiguous lifecycle/subscription questions with procurement/vendor context where appropriate.
* Coach junior engineers; review changes; standardize practices.


**Key Skills And Experience**
* Deep RHEL and/or Rocky Linux (EL8\\+), RPM, dnf/yum, patching and lifecycle.
* Strong container image engineering (Docker/Podman), supply\\-chain and minimal base design.
* Python and shell at a level suitable for maintaining shared automation and tooling.
* CI systems (e.g. Jenkins or equivalent), pipeline design, artifact flows.
* Security: CVE analysis, hardening patterns, guest vs host boundaries.

* Technical leadership, mentoring, stakeholder

title: Lead II - Software Testing (Tableau QA Engineer)
description:
**Role Description**
**Job Title:** 
 Tableau QA Engineer
   

  

**Job Locations:** 
 Bangalore / Hyderabad / Pune / Chennai / Trivandrum / Cochin / Gurugram
   

  

**Role Overview**
 We are seeking a skilled
 **Tableau QA Engineer with Automation experience** 
 to ensure the quality, accuracy, and performance of Tableau dashboards and enterprise reporting solutions. The role focuses on
 **data validation, dashboard testing, and test automation** 
 , supporting large‑scale analytics programs. You will work closely with
 **BI Developers, Data Engineers, QA teams, and business stakeholders** 
 in an Agile environment.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
**Tableau QA \\& Data Validation**
* Perform end‑to‑end testing of Tableau dashboards, reports, and data extracts
* Validate data accuracy, completeness, and consistency between source systems, data warehouses, and Tableau outputs
* Test calculations, filters, parameters, aggregations, and drill‑downs
* Identify data discrepancies, analyze root causes, and raise detailed defects with evidence


**Test Automation**
* Design, develop, and maintain automation test scripts for Tableau dashboards and data validation
* Automate regression testing, data reconciliation, and dashboard validation
* Integrate automation suites with CI/CD pipelines where applicable
* Maintain reusable automation frameworks and test utilities


**Functional \\& Regression Testing**
* Execute functional, regression, and smoke testing for Tableau releases
* Validate dashboard performance, load behavior, and refresh schedules
* Support UAT cycles in collaboration with business users and developers


**Collaboration \\& Reporting**
* Collaborate with BI Developers, Data Engineers, QA, and Product teams
* Participate in sprint planning, reviews, and defect triage meetings
* Provide test status, defect metrics, and quality reports to stakeholders


**Required Skills \\& Qualifications**
* Hands on QA experience with a strong focus on Tableau testing and data validation
* Hands‑on experience with Tableau Desktop, Tableau Server, and published dashboards
* Strong SQL skills for backend data validation and reconciliation
* Experience with test automation tools/frameworks (Selenium, Playwright, custom frameworks, or equivalent)
* Good understanding of data warehousing concepts and ETL/ELT pipelines
* Experience working in Agile / Scrum delivery models


**Skills**
 Tableau, Test automation,data validation,selenium,data testing,playwright,restassured, BI Testing
